notes: Static bad fixtures use expected_decision=fail because the current checker surface treats these defects as fail-class static-analysis findings.
Each case isolates one intended static defect and uses check-skill as the fixture command target.

fixtures.target_rule: Missing SKILL.md frontmatter description must fail static single-skill analysis.
known_limitations: This fixture covers only the missing-description fail branch.

fixtures.target_rule: Missing declared SKILL.md reference must fail static single-skill analysis.
known_limitations: This fixture covers only the missing-reference fail branch.

fixtures.target_rule: Unsupported broad claim text must fail static single-skill analysis.
known_limitations: This fixture covers only the unsupported broad-claim fail branch.

skipped_checks: These fixtures intentionally do not cover boundary, invalid-fixture, or expected-block cases.
These fixtures do not replace the passing good_single_skill or good_suite fixtures.
residual_risk: The fixtures are local negative examples and do not prove broad checker behavior.
Future checker status-vocabulary changes may require refreshing expected_decision values.
claim_boundary: This static bad-fixture manifest covers only the listed negative fixture cases and current local files. It does not prove runtime checker execution, fixture coverage, CLI checks, tests, suite automation, package publication, code-contract validation, release readiness, or future AI behavior without separate current evidence.
